data.content:
I can see the problem. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file in `obj/` isn't automatically picked up.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing expands `Compile` items at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after the file is created. I also need to add it to `FileWrites` for proper clean support.

Let me fix the .csproj file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis and fix:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to `obj/`, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globs expand at *evaluation time* (before any targets execute), so files created during the build in `obj/` aren't automatically included.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target so the generated file is passed to the compiler and properly cleaned on `dotnet clean`.
